'''Zuliria''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] celestial body located in the [[Eucer sector/Legends|Eucer sector]]. It featured several [[City/Legends|cities]] and was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the flying creature known as the [[Ruori]], which most Zulirian urban residents used for transportation. Zuliria hosted the [[Zuliria Museum of Antiquities]], at which the [[Arcona/Legends|Arcona]] [[Ther-das]] at one point worked as an acquisitions director.

After the [[Togorian/Legends|Togorian]] [[Farmer/Legends|farmer]] [[Tyrimm Wyln]] was accidentally [[Death/Legends|killed]] on Zuliria during a [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] raid on a local [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] cell, Ther-das met Wyln's mate, [[Mlatar Thon Gra]], and, after helping him uncover Wyln's fate, invited Thon Gra to become a fellow member of the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]] group of [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weaponry]] afficionados. On Zuliria, Ther-das similarly recruited three other individuals: the [[Ocsin]] [[Cene Gilvent]], the [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] [[Curdik]], and the [[Ka'hren]] [[Kaltor Naklian]].

==Description==
Zuliria was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="AIR" /> situated in the [[Zuliria system/Legends|Zuliria system]], a part of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Eucer sector/Legends|Eucer sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the flying creature known as the [[Ruori]].<ref name="AIR" />

==History==
At one point, the [[Arcona/Legends|Arcona]] [[Ther-das]], a graduate of the [[Lerct Historical Institute/Legends|Lerct Historical Institute]] specializing in ancient [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]], came to work as an acquisitions director for the Zuliria Museum of Antiquities. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], a Rebel cell operated on Zuliria. At some point during that [[Time/Legends|time]], the [[Togorian/Legends|Togorian]] [[Farmer/Legends|farmer]] [[Tyrimm Wyln]] visited the celestial body as part of a tour of a portion of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]]. There, Wyln was caught in cross-fire during an Imperial raid on the local Rebel cell and was [[Death/Legends|killed]].<ref name="AIR" />

[[File:Zulirian_Swordmasters.png|thumb|right|300px|Zuliria was the birthplace of the group known as the Zulirian Swordmasters (founding members Ther-das, Curdik, Kaltor Naklian, and Cene Gilvent pictured).]]
Subsequently, Wyln's mate, the [[bladesmith]] [[Mlatar Thon Gra]] arrived on Zuliria while searching for her. Thon Gra met Ther-das, who offered his assistance in finding Wyln, and eventually the pair's investigation led them to uncover the Togorian's fate. With little tying him to his homeworld, [[Togoria/Legends|Togoria]], Thon Gra then accepted Ther-das' offer to become a founding member of a group of [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapon]] afficionados known as the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]].<ref name="AIR" />

By [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'' represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one [[Standard year/Legends|year]] before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref> the Arcona had also similarly invited three other individuals who were present on Zuliria: the [[Ocsin]] fellow employee of the Zuliria Museum of Antiquities [[Cene Gilvent]], the [[Whiphid/Legends|Whiphid]] wandering [[Sword/Legends|swordsman]] [[Curdik]], and the [[Ka'hren]] [[Scholar/Legends|scholar]] [[Kaltor Naklian]]. The group eventually began challenging and attacking various individuals in the [[Galaxy/Legends|galaxy]] that they believed posed a worthy challenge to their skills, ultimately leading to the Rebel Alliance's [[Task Force on Alliance Security]] compiling [[SecuriDex]] [[Datafile/Legends|datafiles]] on Ther-das, Thon Gra, and Gilvent which all noted Zuliria as their base of operations and also the fact that each of those three individuals posed a high threat level to Alliance operatives on that celestial body.<ref name="AIR" />

==Inhabitants==
The inhabitants of Zuliria's [[City/Legends|cities]] used the local Ruori creatures for transportation. In contrast, the Togorian Mlatar Thon Gra brought his [[mosgoth]] mount, [[Ktlin]], to Zuliria, and Thon Gra flying on Ktlin throughout the Zulirian urban spires made an obvious and fear-inducing sight in the [[Local day/Legends|evening]] light.<ref name="AIR" />

==Locations==
Zuliria featured several cities with spires in them. It also hosted the [[Zuliria Museum of Antiquities]].<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Zuliria was mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="AIR" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' subsequently placed the Zuliria [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square R-7.<ref name="TEA" />
